---
title: "Your Title Here"
author: "Your Name"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: false
    embed-resources: true
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    geometry: margin=2.5cm
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---

# Introduction

[Write your opening paragraph here. Introduce the topic in an engaging way.]

[State your research question clearly here.]

[Briefly explain why this question matters in the Auckland CBD context.]

# Data and Study Area

[Describe the pedestrian dataset here.]

[State the source of the data.]

[Briefly describe the study area and what the sensor network represents.]

[Briefly mention the extra datasets you use, such as weather and geodata.]

## Part A: Data Import and Cleaning

### Loading the pedestrian data

[Explain how you loaded the data and what file you used.]

```{python}
# Insert code for loading the pedestrian data
```

### Inspecting and cleaning the data

[Explain the issues you found, such as note rows, typos, missing values, or formatting problems.]
```{python}
# Insert code for cleaning the data
```

Creating time variables

[Explain the time-related variables you created and why they are useful.]
```{python}
# Insert code for creating month, day_of_week, hour, is_weekend, etc.
```

Reshaping the data

[Explain why you reshaped from wide to long format.]

```{python}
# Insert code for reshaping to long format
```

Summary of the cleaned data

[Write a short summary describing the cleaned dataset.]

```{python}
# Optional: head, shape, summary statistics
```

## Part B: Functions

[Introduce this section by explaining that you wrote reusable functions to support the analysis.]

### Function 1

[Briefly explain what the function does and why it is useful.]
```{python}
# Insert Function 1
```

### Function 2

[Briefly explain what the function does and why it is useful.]
```{python}
# Insert Function 2
```

### Function 3

[Briefly explain what the function does and why it is useful.]
```{python}
# Insert Function 3
```

Part C: API Integration

[Introduce the API you used and explain why it adds something useful to the project.]

Requesting weather data

[Explain what variables you requested and why.]

```{python}
# Insert API request code
```

Processing the API response

[Explain how you turned the response into a usable DataFrame.]

```{python}
# Insert code for processing the API data
```

Merging the API data with pedestrian counts

[Explain how you merged weather with the pedestrian data and what new comparisons this allowed.]

```{python}
# Insert merge code
```

### Analysis

[Use this section to move from prepared data to actual findings.]

#### Overall weekday and weekend comparison

[Write a short paragraph introducing this comparison.]

```{python}
# Insert code for weekday/weekend mean comparison
```

[Write a short interpretation.]

#### Hourly weekday and weekend comparison

[Introduce the hourly comparison and explain why hourly rhythm matters.]

```{python}
# Insert code for hourly summaries
```

[Write a short interpretation.]

#### Sensor-level comparison

[Explain why comparing sensors matters spatially.]
```{python}
# Insert code for sensor-level summaries
```

[Write a short interpretation.]

#### Weather comparison

[Explain why comparing wet and dry conditions matters.]

```{python}
# Insert code for weather summaries
```

[Write a short interpretation.]

#### Optional extra analysis: clustering

[Introduce the clustering analysis and explain why you added it.]

```{python}
# Insert code for weekday hourly clustering
```

[Write a short interpretation of what the clusters suggest.]

## Part D: Visualisations

[Introduce the visualisations section and explain that each figure reveals a different pattern.]

### Figure 1: Weekday vs weekend hourly pattern

[Briefly introduce the first figure.]

```{python}
# Insert code for the weekday/weekend hourly line chart
```

[Write 2–4 sentences interpreting the figure.]

### Figure 2: Weather effect on foot traffic

[Briefly introduce the second figure.]

```{python}
# Insert code for the wet/dry weekday/weekend bar chart
```

[Write 2–4 sentences interpreting the figure.]

### Figure 3: Sensor differences across the CBD

[Briefly introduce the third figure.]

```{python}
# Insert code for the top/bottom sensor difference bar chart

```

[Write 2–4 sentences interpreting the figure.]

### Figure 4: Geospatial visualisation

[Explain what the map shows and why it is useful.]

[If you are using a screenshot in the QMD/PDF, insert it here.]

[If you want to keep the interactive map creation code in the QMD, put it below.]

```{python}
# Optional: insert code that creates and saves the interactive map
```

[Write 2–4 sentences interpreting the map.]

### Optional Figure 5: Cluster profiles

[Introduce the cluster profile chart.]

```{python}
# Insert code for the cluster profile line chart
```

[Write a short interpretation.]

### Optional Figure 6: Cluster map

[Introduce the cluster map if you decide to keep it.]

```{python}
# Optional: insert code that creates and saves the cluster map
```

## Key Findings

[Summarise the main findings in plain language.]

[You can structure this as a few short paragraphs or a concise list in prose.]

## Limitations

[Write about the main limitations of the project.]

[Possible ideas:
	•	one year of data only
	•	weather measured at one city-level location
	•	sensor points do not represent true catchment areas
	•	clustering is exploratory
	•	interactive maps simplified to screenshots in the PDF/blog
]

## Conclusion

[Write a short concluding paragraph that directly answers your research question.]

## Reproducibility

[State where the reader can find the project files.]

GitHub repository: [Paste your link here]

[Explain what is included in the repository:
	•	QMD file
	•	data files
	•	environment information
	•	interactive HTML maps
]

## Data Sources / References

[Heart of the City pedestrian counts]

[Open-Meteo API]

[Any other sources you want to cite]
